components.description: Receives telemetry via the OpenTelemetry Protocol over gRPC and/or HTTP.

protocols.description: At least one protocol must be enabled.

endpoint.description: host:port to listen on for gRPC.

max_recv_msg_size_mib.description: Maximum gRPC message size in MiB.

endpoint.description: host:port to listen on for HTTP.

components.description: Receives trace data in Jaeger format (gRPC, Thrift HTTP, Thrift compact/binary UDP).
Receives spans in Zipkin v1/v2 format.

parse_string_tags.description: Parse string tags into typed values.

components.description: Scrapes metrics from Prometheus-instrumented targets using standard scrape_configs.
Collects host-level metrics: CPU, memory, disk, filesystem, network, load, processes.

root_path.description: Host filesystem root when running in a container.

scrapers.description: Enable individual scrapers (cpu, memory, disk, filesystem, network, load, paging, processes, process).

components.description: Tails and parses log files from disk.

include.description: Glob patterns of files to read.

exclude.description: Glob patterns of files to skip.

operators.description: Stanza parsing operators applied in order.

components.description: Collects metrics about files on disk: size, modification time, access time.

include.description: Glob pattern of files to observe.

metrics.description: Enable/disable individual metrics (file.size, file.mtime, file.atime, file.ctime, file.count).

components.description: Consumes telemetry from Kafka topics.

topic.description: Topic to consume (defaults per signal, e.g. otlp_spans).

components.description: Receives syslog messages over TCP or UDP (RFC 3164 / RFC 5424).
Receives raw log lines over TCP.
Collects container metrics from the Docker daemon.
Pulls pod, container and node metrics from the kubelet API.
Collects cluster-level metrics and entity events from the Kubernetes API server.
Performs synthetic HTTP checks against endpoints and reports availability metrics.
Collects metrics from a Redis server via INFO.
Collects metrics from a PostgreSQL server.
Batches telemetry before export to reduce outgoing connections. Recommended in every pipeline.

timeout.description: Send batch after this duration regardless of size.

send_batch_max_size.description: Upper bound on batch size; 0 means no limit.

components.description: Drops or refuses data when memory usage exceeds limits. Place first in each pipeline.

spike_limit_mib.description: Spike allowance in MiB (default 20% of limit_mib).

limit_percentage.description: Alternative to limit_mib: percentage of total memory.

components.description: Inserts, updates, deletes or hashes attributes on spans, metrics and logs.
Applies attribute changes to resource attributes.
Detects resource information from the host environment (system, env, cloud providers, k8s).
Drops telemetry matching OTTL conditions or include/exclude rules.
Modifies telemetry using OTTL statements.
Samples whole traces after completion based on latency, status, attributes and rate policies.
Head-samples a fixed percentage of traces or logs.
Enriches telemetry with Kubernetes pod, namespace and node metadata.
Regroups telemetry by resource attributes; also compacts duplicate resources.
Converts cumulative sum/histogram metrics to delta temporality.
Renames metrics and labels, aggregates and scales values.
Renames spans and extracts attributes from span names.
Deletes or masks attribute values that are not on an allowlist or that match blocked patterns.
Exports telemetry via OTLP over gRPC.

auth.description: Authenticate using an auth extension (e.g. basicauth, bearertokenauth, oauth2client).

authenticator.description: ID of the auth extension to use.

components.description: Exports telemetry via OTLP over HTTP (protobuf or JSON).

auth.description: Authenticate using an auth extension (e.g. basicauth, bearertokenauth, oauth2client).

authenticator.description: ID of the auth extension to use.

components.description: Logs telemetry to the console. Deprecated — use the debug exporter instead.
Prints telemetry to the console for debugging. Replaces the logging exporter.
Exports traces to Jaeger via gRPC. Removed — Jaeger natively accepts OTLP; use the otlp exporter instead.
Exposes metrics on an HTTP endpoint for Prometheus to scrape.
Pushes metrics to a Prometheus remote-write compatible backend.

auth.description: Authenticate using an auth extension (e.g. basicauth, bearertokenauth, oauth2client).

authenticator.description: ID of the auth extension to use.

components.description: Publishes telemetry to Kafka topics.
Writes telemetry to a file as JSON or protobuf.
Consistently routes telemetry to a pool of downstream collectors by trace ID or service name.

auth.description: Authenticate using an auth extension (e.g. basicauth, bearertokenauth, oauth2client).

authenticator.description: ID of the auth extension to use.

components.description: Stores telemetry in an Amazon S3 bucket.
Writes telemetry to ClickHouse tables.
Writes telemetry to Elasticsearch.

auth.description: Authenticate using an auth extension (e.g. basicauth, bearertokenauth, oauth2client).

authenticator.description: ID of the auth extension to use.

components.description: Sends telemetry to Splunk via the HTTP Event Collector.

auth.description: Authenticate using an auth extension (e.g. basicauth, bearertokenauth, oauth2client).

authenticator.description: ID of the auth extension to use.

components.description: Exposes an HTTP endpoint reporting collector liveness, for load balancer / k8s probes.
Serves Go pprof profiling data for performance debugging.
Serves live zPages (tracez, pipelinez) for in-process diagnostics.
Pre-allocates heap to reduce GC churn. Removed — use GOMEMLIMIT instead.
Provides HTTP Basic authentication for receivers (server) or exporters (client).
Attaches or verifies a static bearer token for gRPC/HTTP connections.

filename.description: Read the token from this file instead.

components.description: Performs OAuth2 client-credentials flows for exporter authentication.
Sets outgoing request headers, optionally propagated from incoming request context.
Persists component state to disk — enables durable sending queues and checkpoints.
Forwards data unchanged from one pipeline to another of the same signal type.
Counts spans, data points or log records and emits the counts as metrics.
Aggregates R.E.D metrics (request rate, errors, duration) from span data.
Builds service-dependency graph metrics from traces.
Routes telemetry to different pipelines based on OTTL conditions on resource attributes.
Generates metrics and logs from span exception events.
